speaker: MATT
raw_text: There are a number of coins in there. There's probably a good, hefty pile of gold, slightly molded by the moisture that's been kept inside this chest until it was detonated. Gathering it up, you find approximately 420 gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know where it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The familiar whine of the trap catches your ear. You dodge out of the way instinctively. Your sister's not so lucky. You take the brunt of the damage, but not the full damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no. Did I destroy the loot?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to inspect what the chest once was. As you go back to where the chest was, most of the chest is destroyed. It's knocked off its hinge. What you do find inside, though, is what looks like a very, very... There we go. What looks like a slightly-charred two-handed blade: a giant sword that is currently laid inside. Looks like it's been there a while in storage. There was a set of natural armor that's been completely destroyed by the blast.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The grapple does not give you advantage, no. So you have disadvantage on the roll, technically, because you are scared of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nothing. She's going to wait it out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you're taking your turn after Laura?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes. I'll follow suit. I'm going to... I'm going to straight-up cast this because I have to. I cast Telekinesis and I pull the eye out. (sucking noise)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Interesting! Okay. So Clarota, at this time, is also going to attempt a Mind Blast on the creature. It moves to the back of the boat and while it's currently grappled, he's going to attempt that. The saving throw does succeed, so it only takes half damage. Okay. Marking that. And then as you do Telekinesis. Roll a d20 plus five.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Okay. And do I have advantage because of the grapple?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I'll give you a detailed description. Essentially, this pyramid comes to a top and then flattens up at the very top. Where you would expect there to be a flat surface is instead, essentially, a windowed opening. You don't think it's purely open like you could just jump through. There is some sort of glass that coats it, but there is a brass cross-beam at the very top, and then part of this cross-beam holds aloft this strange series of metallic wires that spiral and intertwine that serves as a conical focus piece. You can't see the interior of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a magic fun bag. It's probably got everything from Silence and Dispel Magic to Petrify to a disintegration ray.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're thinking about this wrong, though. You're thinking about this in the wrong direction. Beholder is not-- that's not the goal.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Even more than that, it's the hundreds of allies we could have.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Exactly! We free the Elder Brain, they attack it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Not the beholder. If we can free the Elder Brain--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We don't have to fight it ourselves.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Then we're not alone. Now, what was creating this green light? Where was it emanating from?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It seemed to be emanating from the top opening of this pyramid that was the main temple of the center of town.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can you describe the top of the pyramid?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: TFdiggs donated 20 bucks. "So nice. Had to pick up the shirt twice." Aww. Good man. "Always look forward to having all the loins girded and all the violence encouraged. Thank you for being awesome."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Browncoat2511 also donated $100. "Thanks for inspiring our friends to finally play D&D with us. I'm donating as a gift for my wife, Lauren."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "For her 30th birthday. She's an avid reader, our D&D group's bard, and a big fan of the show."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. ZandorInvictus donated $75 to 826. "#LessThanThree you guys. Excited that a week from today I get to play my first game in like 7 years. Hope to do as well as you guys." That's awesome! Zandor, have fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zofer donated $25. "Many thanks for all the time and effort you put into making Critical Role." Well, thank you guys for watching it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're glad you guys like it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Not a bad idea. We talked about doing it for the channel going forward, so keep an eye on that. This is really warm. I'm putting the hood down. Electricidolon. 20 bucks. "Back again this week. Got a shirt this time, too. Thank you for giving me something amazing to look forward to every week, guys. Need a Critical Role animated series. For serious." I agree. That'd be cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We still need to fix that. Yeah I'm half-elven.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We just need to fix that in the opening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: On your mother's side or your father's side?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Gil Ramirez! 10 bucks. "Hey guys, so glad to have met you at Ren Faire." Oh, hey! What's up, Gil? (all cheer)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Met him a few weeks back. "Let me know if you guys want those brooches made."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, yeah. Those was awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Kavemtg. "First time watching live after obsessively consuming the videos for 2 weeks. Fuck time difference." Yeah. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I had to go for the holy trinity tee, sub, and donation." That's awesome. "Much deserved. Thanks for everything, guys. K. P.S: Matt, we need DM tips column/video series."

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Argh. Mahidal, who I've met on the Minecraft servers. Awesome. Donated five bucks. "Oh man, the Critical Role crew, you remember that time we all went to Ancient Rome and they had pizza and lasers? And then we found out the Doctor was there and someone had messed with the timeline? That was a good day, and we all escaped!" (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, they sent a message and asked for everyone's shirt sizes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Why are you guys so awesome? Oh my god. Okay, NorSarah. Four bucks. Awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I would like to rage! Or donate, in this case. I love your show. It's my most favorite thing in the world. I'm joining my own D&D group because of it." "Thanks for sharing your adventures with us. Heart. And remember, Keyleth: fish are friends, not food."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: G1bigdaddyheft. 25 bucks. "TV shows can't come close to the epic power of Critical Role Thursdays. As always, thank you all for coming together and sharing your adventure with us and inspiring so many people to begin our own." Thank you, guys. That's amazing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: To 826LA charity. "Critical Role has been the most awesome show I've discovered on Twitch. Thank you for putting this together." Thank you. That's incredible.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Entschuldige. I don't know what that is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Danke schön. Did I say that right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You said thank you, he said excuse me, he's saying good morning. I said see you later, see you next time, ta-ta.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: e3kguru, $25. "Thanking Geek & Sundry for shows like Critical Role. To the cast, thanks for taking the time every week to bring us into your world. Much love to everyone and happy tenth episode." Thank you so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Lukegoodatstuff, 20 bucks. "I didn't expect Critical Role to be my favorite show on the channel, but here we are. Less than three you all. It's been a fun ten episodes. Looking forward to many more."
